channel.title: Al Jazeera – Breaking News, World News and Video from Al Jazeera
channel.description: Breaking News, World News and Video from Al Jazeera

image.title: Al Jazeera – Breaking News, World News and Video from Al Jazeera
image.description: Breaking News, World News and Video from Al Jazeera

item.title: ‘Strategic doctrine’: Iran hails military shift after Beirut raid response
item.description: Iranians describe attacks on Israel as part of military approach prioritising 'taking initiative and offensive power'.

item.title: Lebanon’s children face physical, psychological trauma amid Israel’s war
item.description: Experts warn of lasting trauma for Lebanon's children as war continues to shatter their sense of safety.

item.title: Lebanon Latest: Israel pushes deeper as conflict escalates
item.description: The latest developments from southern Lebanon amid escalating Israeli attacks.

item.title: ‘Lives turned in a second’: Family of baby Sam, shot dead by Israel, grieve
item.description: A father speaks of the moment his seven-month-old Palestinian son was shot dead in the West Bank by an Israeli soldier.

item.title: IAEA chief says Iran-US nuclear talks in ‘complicated phase’
item.description: IAEA chief says Iran-US nuclear talks in 'complicated phase'.

item.title: Why was Israel spared scrutiny for the 1967 USS Liberty attack?
item.description: US Representative Massie set to honour the crew of the vessel on the House floor, bringing attention to the 1967 attack.

item.title: Meta to take legal action against Israeli spyware company NSO
item.description: WhatsApp disrupted phishing attempts linked to NSO, blacklisted by the US for security concerns.

item.title: How extreme weather and heat could affect players at World Cup 2026
item.description: Climate experts warn that 25% of World Cup games could be played in very hot conditions, affecting fans and players.

item.title: Texas-bound jet crashes during emergency landing in Dominican Republic
item.description: Texas-bound private jet crashes during emergency landing in Dominican Republic.

item.title: Al Jazeera explains China-North Korea ties as Xi visits Pyongyang
item.description: China’s Xi Jinping is visiting North Korea for the first time in seven years.

item.title: At least 11 killed in Pakistan-administered Kashmir clashes before rally
item.description: Police crack down down on supporters of the Joint Awami Action Committee, which plans to hold a rally Tuesday.

item.title: The retaking of Cuba
item.description: Decades-old property claims, frozen since the 1960s, are being turned into live instruments of reconquest.

item.title: North Korea needs China for survival: Why does Beijing need Pyongyang?
item.description: Chinese President Xi Jinping visits North Korea as Beijing aims to boost ties amid growing Pyongyang-Moscow relations.

item.title: Smoke rises in illegal Israeli settlement after Iranian missile attack
item.description: Smoke was seen in an illegal Israeli settlement in the occupied West Bank after Iran launched launched missiles.

item.title: Video: Israel claims strike on Iranian petrochemical complex
item.description: Video shows plumes of smoke rising from Mahshahr Petrochemical Complex in Iran’s Khuzestan Province on Monday.

item.title: Which European nation has the best chance to win World Cup 2026?
item.description: Europe has the most World Cup winners in the event's history, but what are the continent's chances at the 2026 edition?

item.title: Armenian PM Pashinyan declares victory in parliamentary elections
item.description: Armenian PM Pashinyan declares victory in parliamentary elections

item.title: Could ex-ISIL fighters be used against Iran, as a Russian official claimed?
item.description: Russia’s Federal Security Service chief said Western powers could weaponise ex-fighters, but analysts doubt the claim.

item.title: Ohio police drag away football pitch invader in pre-World Cup friendly
item.description: US police were filmed dragging away a pitch-invading fan during a friendly football match between Ecuador and Guatemala.

item.title: Iran official says US to blame for resumption of hostilities
item.description: Iran’s FM spokesman Esmaeil Baghaei said Israel’s ally the US is responsible for ‘whatever happens in the region'.

item.title: Iran and Israel bomb each other: Is the ceasefire over?
item.description: Analysts say both sides are pushing the ceasefire to its limits while seeking greater leverage.

item.title: Pro-Western PM Pashinyan sees off Russian pressure to win Armenia election
item.description: Nikol Pashinyan's Civil ​Contract party retains power with 49.81 percent in closely-watched election.

item.title: Israel kills 14 people in attacks across Gaza
item.description: Israel killed at least 14 people in Gaza on Sunday in strikes across Khan Younis, Gaza City, and Deir el-Balah.

item.title: Philippines earthquake kills 15, prompts tsunami fears: All to know
item.description: The latest tremors come eight months after the Philippines suffered its strongest earthquake in 12 years.

item.title: China’s Xi Jinping arrives in North Korea on rare state visit
item.description: A cheering crowd dressed in festive attire welcomes the Chinese leader at Pyongyang's main square.
